title: Definition of Al-Faʿil (the Subject of a Verb)

sections.text: Al-faʿil (the doer/subject of a verb) is a noun in the nominative case (marfuʿ) that indicates the one from whom the action proceeds, or with whom the action is associated.
Examples:

items.text: — Zayd stood. (Here al-faʿil is Zayd, because the action of standing proceeded from him.)
— Muhammad fell ill. (Here al-faʿil is Muhammad, with whom the illness was associated — i.e., he did not fall ill by his own will.)

sections.text: Types of Al-Faʿil
Al-faʿil is divided into two kinds:
1 — Manifest (zahir), e.g.:

items.text: — Muhammad stood / stands. (Here al-faʿil is Muhammad — singular masculine.)
— The two teachers stood / stand. (Here al-faʿil is 'al-muʿallimani' — dual masculine.)
— The diligent ones stood / stand. (Here al-faʿil is 'al-mujtahiduna' — sound masculine plural.)
— The men stood / stand. (Here al-faʿil is 'al-rijal' — broken plural.)
— Hind stood / stands. (Here al-faʿil is Hind — singular feminine.)
— The two girls stood / stand. (Here al-faʿil is 'al-fatatani' — dual feminine.)
— The nurses stood / stand. (Here al-faʿil is 'al-mumarridat' — sound feminine plural.)
— The Hinds stood / stand. (Here al-faʿil is 'al-hunud' — broken plural of Hind.)
— Your father stood / stands. (Here al-faʿil is 'abu-ka' — one of the Five Nouns.)
— My teacher stood / stands. (Here al-faʿil is 'muʿallim-i' — annexed to the speaker's yaʾ.)

sections.text: 2 — Implicit (mudmar — a pronoun), e.g.:

items.text: — I struck. (Here al-faʿil is the taʾ — a connected pronoun in the position of nominative.)
— We struck. (Here al-faʿil is 'na' — a connected pronoun fixed on sukun, in the position of nominative.)
— You two struck. (Here al-faʿil is the taʾ — a connected pronoun fixed on damma, in the position of nominative; the 'ma' is the sign of duality.)
— You — masc. plural — struck. (Here al-faʿil is the taʾ; the mim is the sign of plural.)
— You — fem. plural — struck. (Here al-faʿil is the taʾ; the nun is the sign of plural.)

sections.text: Iʿrab of Al-Faʿil

items.text: — The man came.

sections.text:
jaʾa: a past-tense verb fixed on fatha.
al-rajul: al-faʿil in the nominative, marked by the manifest damma at its end.

items.text: — The two friends raced.

sections.text:
tasabaqa: a past-tense verb fixed on fatha.
al-sadiqani: al-faʿil in the nominative, marked by alif because it is dual; the nun substitutes for the tanwin of the singular noun.

items.text: — The players stood.

sections.text:
qama: a past-tense verb fixed on fatha.
al-laʿibun: al-faʿil in the nominative, marked by waw because it is a sound masculine plural.

items.text: — Your brother went.

sections.text:
dhahaba: a past-tense verb fixed on fatha.
akh-u-ka: al-faʿil in the nominative, marked by waw because it is one of the Five Nouns; the kaf is a connected pronoun fixed on (its position) in the position of genitive as a mudaf ilayh.
Definition of Naʾib al-Faʿil (the Deputy Subject)

Naʾib al-faʿil (the deputy subject) is the noun in the nominative case that takes the place of the omitted subject (al-faʿil) and assumes its rulings. Its verb is changed to the form fuʿila in the past and yufʿalu in the present, and the verb is then called mabni li-l-majhul (the passive voice).
Example:

items.text: — Muhammad ate the food.

sections.text: Where is al-faʿil?
Al-faʿil is Muhammad; al-taʿam is the direct object.

items.text: — The food was eaten.

sections.text: Where is al-faʿil?
It is omitted, and 'al-taʿam' has taken its place [as naʾib al-faʿil].
The Passive-Voice Verb

We said that naʾib al-faʿil requires the verb to be in the passive voice. The form requires giving its first letter a damma and the letter before its last a kasra if it is past-tense, e.g., duriba al-ghulam ('the boy was struck'), ukila al-taʿam ('the food was eaten'), quriʾa al-Qurʾan ('the Quran was recited').

— And giving its first letter a damma and the letter before its last a fatha if it is present-tense, e.g., yuʿaqabu al-mujrim ('the criminal is punished'), yuʾjaru al-sabir ('the patient one is rewarded'), yukarramu al-najih ('the successful one is honored').
Types of Naʾib al-Faʿil
Naʾib al-faʿil, like al-faʿil, is divided into two kinds:
1 — Manifest (zahir), e.g.:

items.text: — The food was eaten. (singular)
— The two boys were struck. (dual)
— The criminals were punished. (sound masculine plural)
— Your brother was struck. (Five Nouns)

sections.text: And so on with similar examples — the matter is, I think, clear.

2 — Implicit (mudmar — a pronoun), e.g.: buʿith-tu ('I was sent'), buʿith-na ('we were sent'), buʿith-ta ('you — masc. — were sent'), buʿith-ti ('you — fem. — were sent'), buʿith-u ('they were sent'), buʿith-na ('they — fem. — were sent'), and so on.
Iʿrab of Naʾib al-Faʿil

items.text: — I was struck.

sections.text: A past-tense passive verb, fixed on sukun. The taʾ is naʾib al-faʿil — a connected pronoun fixed on damma, in the position of nominative.

items.text: — You two were struck.

sections.text: A past-tense passive verb, fixed on sukun. The taʾ is naʾib al-faʿil — a connected pronoun fixed on damma, in the position of nominative; the mim and the alif are the sign of duality.

items.text: — It was eaten.

sections.text: A past-tense passive verb. Naʾib al-faʿil is a permissibly concealed pronoun, implied as 'huwa'.

items.text: — Your brother was struck.

sections.text:
duriba: a past-tense passive verb fixed on fatha.
akh-u-ka: naʾib al-faʿil in the nominative, marked by waw because it is one of the Five Nouns; the kaf is a connected pronoun fixed on (its position) in the position of genitive as a mudaf ilayh.

items.text: — The trustworthy one is recognized by his honesty.

sections.text:
yuʿrafu: a present-tense passive verb.
al-amin: naʾib al-faʿil in the nominative, marked by the manifest damma at its end.
bi-l-sidq: a prepositional phrase (jarr wa-majrur).
